differential_diagnoses: Ectopic variceal hemorrhage secondary to portal vein stenosis
Hemobilia due to hepaticojejunostomy complications or biliary stricture
Portal biliopathy with associated portal hypertension
next_steps: Interventional radiology consultation for portal venography and pressure measurement
Mesenteric angiography to look for occult vascular anomalies or variceal bleeding source
Deep enteroscopy (balloon-assisted) to specifically target the jejuno-jejunostomy site
Red blood cell scintigraphy to localize intermittent active bleeding
Multidisciplinary evaluation with hepatobiliary surgery regarding the hepaticojejunostomy and vascular anatomy

differential_diagnoses: Hemobilia secondary to hepatic artery pseudoaneurysm at the hepaticojejunostomy site
Ectopic variceal hemorrhage (jejunal varices) secondary to portal vein stenosis
Portal hypertensive biliopathy with associated vascular abnormalities
Dieulafoy's lesion in the excluded limb of the jejunum
next_steps: Perform hepatic artery angiography with potential embolization to rule out and treat a pseudoaneurysm
Perform portal venography with pressure measurements to assess the hemodynamic significance of the portal stenosis
Consult interventional radiology for evaluation of the perihilar enhancement and venous stenosis
Perform balloon-assisted deep enteroscopy to directly visualize the hepaticojejunostomy site and the Roux-en-Y limb
Schedule a multidisciplinary team conference involving hepatobiliary surgery and interventional radiology to discuss definitive vascular or biliary reconstruction
